title: monarda fistulosa subsp. menthifolia
intro: Monarda fistulosa subsp. menthifolia, also known as bearded horsemint, is a subspecies of the monarda fistulosa plant, a type of flowering perennial native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of essential oils, particularly menthol and limonene. The health benefits of bearded horsemint include relief from digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote respiratory well-being. Bearded horsemint can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
